regime_read: ES & NQ moving together — broad agreement, more DURABLE directional signal. Trust the direction more.
narrative:
Macro backdrop —
[2026-07-01] US economic state (FRED):
  Growth: real GDP 2.1% (q/q ann)
  Inflation: CPI 3.67% YoY | core CPI 2.26% | core PCE 2.89% (Fed target 2%)
  Labor: unemployment 4.3%
  Rates: fed funds 3.63% | 10Y 4.38% | 10Y-2Y 0.3% (NORMAL)
  Consumer: UMich sentiment 44.8 | VIX 17.65

---
PRE-US-OPEN BRIEFING — ES/NQ, as of 2026-07-01 12:15 UTC. Note upfront: this is intelligence, not a validated edge. My own scorecard is n=10 (0.9 hit, 0.3 regime acc) — still CALIBRATING, so weight this as a structured read, not gospel.

1) MOVE-BY-MOVE READ. Both indices are mildly red on the day (ES -0.27%, NQ -0.60%) but solidly green on the week (ES +1.34%, NQ +2.80%). The picture is a strong up-week taking a breather. Yesterday's session pulled back off prior-day highs (ES 7567.75, NQ 30599.75) into current 7527.5 / 30339.75 — a normal give-back after an extended run, not a trend break. NQ's larger daily drop vs ES fits the 1-day spread (-0.00327): tech gave back slightly more, likely mild profit-taking in the leadership names into month/quarter-start rebalancing. The dominant catalyst backdrop is geopolitics-heavy (multiple Iran/US headlines, all tagged BOTH) plus recurring AI-capex/tech-earnings chatter on the NQ side. None of the news is a hard, market-moving surprise — it's ambient risk, not a clean directional trigger. RSI (ES 64.8, NQ 63.0) is firm-but-not-extreme; room to run without being stretched. Price sits right on session VWAP (ES 7527.1 vs last 7527.5; NQ VWAP 30383 vs last 30340 — NQ just below), consistent with a two-sided, balancing tape.

2) VOLUME-PROFILE READ. ES: POC 7552.49, value area 7389.63–7557.43, HVNs stacked at 7552/7498/7464/7424/7419. NQ: POC 30535.39, value area 29268.09–30568.74, HVNs at 30535/30035/29601. Read: on BOTH instruments price is currently trading BELOW the POC and near the upper third of the value area — i.e. we sold off from the high-volume acceptance zone but are still inside value. The 5-day accumulation shelf sits lower (ES ~7419–7464, NQ ~29601–30035). For NQ, 30035 is the pivotal HVN — it's the top of the deeper accumulation zone and the logical line in the sand: hold above it and the up-week structure stays intact; lose it and price is falling back toward the mid-value/lower shelf. POC 30535 is the magnet on the upside — reclaiming it flips momentum back to leadership-buyers. Limitation acknowledged: this is volume-AT-price (where trade occurred), NOT order-flow delta. I cannot see buy/sell aggression, so treat these as structural reference levels, not proof of who's in control.

3) NQ/ES LEAD-LAG REGIME. Contemporaneous correlation 0.943, lag-0 corr 0.882, no coherent leader (best_lag corr only -0.092, leader NONE), divergence FALSE → clean BREADTH regime. ES and NQ are moving together, which historically makes the directional signal more DURABLE and more trustworthy than a concentration/single-leader tape. Implication: when both move as one, you can size the directional read with more conviction and less fear of a hidden divergence trap. Caveat: my regime-classification accuracy is only 0.3 on a tiny sample, so I trust the raw correlation math here more than my historical labeling.

4) EVENT CALENDAR. TODAY: ISM Manufacturing PMI at 10:00 ET — reduce size into the print; this can whip the open-to-mid-morning tape. In 2 days (07-03): Nonfarm Payrolls, 8:30 ET, high-impact — widen stops / size down going in. CPI sits 11 days out. So the near-term is bracketed by two data events (ISM today, NFP Friday). These are CADENCE ESTIMATES, not verified release dates — verify against the official calendar before acting. Net: this is a size-DOWN, respect-the-print week, not a swing-for-the-fences week.

5) SYNTHESIS. Meta-regime: BROAD UPTREND IN A HEALTHY PULLBACK, inside value, correlated (breadth), but capped by event risk (ISM today, NFP Fri). Structure: both indices well above SMA200 (ES +8.06%, NQ +15.86%), strong weekly returns, RSI firm not extreme, price holding inside value area above the deeper accumulation shelf. That argues for a LONG-the-dip lean. Working against it: today's mild red, NQ trading just below VWAP and below POC, ambient geopolitical noise, and two data events that demand reduced size. News is NOT clean (geopolitics is ambient, no decisive catalyst) even though the lead-lag IS clean — that mixed picture keeps me at MODERATE confidence, not high. BIAS: long-the-dip, ~58%, favoring buyers while NQ holds above the 30035 HVN and ES holds inside value. INVALIDATION: NQ close/acceptance below 30035 flips this to neutral/cautious (structure breaks back toward the lower shelf 29601 and below). Execution note: ES/NQ is research only here — no execution path (CME futures; OANDA is forex-only) — treat as directional intelligence and size DOWN into today's 10:00 ET ISM and Friday's NFP.
